DECLARATION OF COUNSEL IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANYS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO TRANSFER TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY (CHANGE OF VENUE) (TRANSACTION ID # 60770370) FILED BY DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY (CHANGE OF VENUE) IS DENIED. FORD HAS NOT SHOWN THAT SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY IS THE WRONG VENUE UNDER CCP 396B(A). UNDER CCP 395(A), A NONRESIDENT DEFENDANT MAY BE SUED IN ANY COUNTY. FORD ADMITS THAT ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IS NOT IN CALIFORNIA, BUT IN DEARBORN, MICHIGAN. WHILE FORD HAS AN AGENT FOR SERVICE OF PROCESS LOCATED IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY, CCP 395.5, THE VENUE PROVISION FOR CORPORATIONS ONLY REFERS TO THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF A CORPORATION. CONSEQUENTLY, BECAUSE FORD IS SUBJECT TO VENUE IN ANY CALIFORNIA COUNTY, PLAINTIFF RANDALL MANGALI'S CHOICE OF SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY AS THE VENUE FOR HIS ACTION AGAINST FORD WAS NOT IMPROPER. FURTHERMORE, EVEN IF FORD'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS WAS IN CALIFORNIA, A CORPORATION MAY CHANGE THE VENUE ONLY TO ITS COUNTY OF RESIDENCE, AND THERE IS NO AUTHORITY FOR CHANGING IT TO SOME OTHER COUNTY IN WHICH THE PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE BROUGHT THE ACTION. BEUTKE V AMERICAN SECURITIES CO., 132 CAL APP.2D 354 (1955). THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR NO. 12353, EMAIL: MVISCIO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint is denied. The fraud and concealment causes of action support plaintiffs' request for punitive damages.

Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant Ford Motor Company's demurrer to the concealment, intentional misrepresentation, and negligent misrepresentation causes of action is overruled in its entirety. The negligent and intentional misrepresentation causes of action are adequately alleged. While plaintiffs' vague reference to Ford's brochures and advertising is insufficient to support a claim for misrepresentation, plaintiffs also allege that Ford falsely represented that the transmission was "automatic" when it was not. Paragraph 70 of the complaint alleges that the representation was made on the window sticker. This allegation is sufficiently particular. Ford's argument that the transmission was in fact "automatic" is a contested factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Plaintiffs plead a viable concealment claim based on active concealment. (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 65; Complaint 58-59.) The complaint does not facially disclose that any of the claims are time-barred. Plaintiffs sufficiently plead delayed discovery at paragraph 85. Whether their discovery was truly delayed is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. California applies the American Pipe tolling doctrine. Paragraphs 98-106 of the complaint support tolling. (Falk v. Children's Hospital of Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1454, 1466-1468.) Ford's economic loss rule argument fails because plaintiffs have alleged viable fraud claims.

Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer To Contra Costa County (Change Of Venue)
Off Calendar Jul-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David A. Serrano of the law offices of Gordon Rees LLP (415.986.5900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Ford Motor Company

No other appearance is made in this matter

Court hears from moving party counsel as reflected in the record of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion To Transfer To Contra Costa County (Change Of Venue) is DENIED. Ford has not shown that San Francisco County is the wrong venue under CCP 396b(a). Under CCP 395(a), a nonresident defendant may be sued in any county. Ford admits that its principal place of business is not in California, but in Dearborn, Michigan. While Ford has an agent for service of process located in Los Angeles County, CCP 395.5, the venue provision for corporations only refers to the principal place of business of a corporation. Consequently, because Ford is subject to venue in any California county, plaintiff Randall Mangali's choice of San Francisco County as the venue for his action against Ford was not improper. Furthermore, even if Ford's principal place of business was in California, a corporation may change the venue only to its county of residence, and there is no authority for changing it to some other county in which the plaintiff could have brought the action. Beutke v American Securities Co., 132 Cal App.2d 354 (1955).

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
